hero.label: Environmental Nutrition Intelligence
hero.heading: Nourishing People. Sustaining the Planet.
hero.subtitle: NourishSphere is building the world's first platform that unifies nutritional science, environmental impact, and clinical health outcomes — making integrated food intelligence actionable for everyone.

problemPreview.label: THE PROBLEM
problemPreview.heading: A $20 Trillion Crisis Hiding in Plain Sight
problemPreview.description: Poor nutrition and its environmental consequences represent one of the largest solvable crises of our time.
problemPreview.stat1_description: Economic burden of poor nutrition and its cascading health and environmental impacts
problemPreview.stat2_description: Attributable to poor-quality diets — the single largest modifiable risk factor for disease
problemPreview.stat3_description: Of greenhouse gas emissions come from the food system

whatWeDo.label: WHAT WE DO
whatWeDo.heading: One Platform. Three Audiences.
whatWeDo.description: NourishSphere serves everyone from individuals making daily food choices to researchers studying the nutrition-environment nexus and policymakers crafting integrated guidelines.
whatWeDo.individuals_description: Make informed food choices with real data on nutrition quality, environmental cost, and health impact — all in one place.
whatWeDo.researchers_description: Access integrated datasets spanning 5,000+ foods, 150+ nutrients, and 18-category life-cycle assessment data.
whatWeDo.policymakers_description: Model dietary scenarios with unified nutrition-environment-health data to craft evidence-based guidelines.

gap.label: THE GAP
gap.heading: No Existing Tool Connects the Full Picture
gap.description: Current tools address fragments of the problem. NourishSphere is the first to integrate all three dimensions.

latestNews.label: LATEST
latestNews.heading: News & Updates
latestNews.placeholder1_excerpt: The world's first integrated nutrition-environment-health platform begins beta testing with 1,000 users across Canada.
latestNews.placeholder2_title: New Research: Linking Diet Quality to Environmental Cost
latestNews.placeholder2_excerpt: Our latest peer-reviewed study quantifies the relationship between dietary quality scores and monetized environmental impact.
latestNews.placeholder3_excerpt: New collaboration brings advanced DALY-based health impact modeling to the NourishSphere platform.

cta.heading: Join the Movement for Smarter, Sustainable Nutrition
cta.description: Whether you're a researcher, policymaker, or simply someone who cares about the future of food — there's a place for you.

footer.about_description: NourishSphere is a Quebec-registered non-profit building the world's first environmental nutrition intelligence platform.

problem.hero_description: No single system exists that unifies nutritional science, environmental life-cycle impact, and clinical health outcomes into one decision framework — leaving individuals powerless, research fragmented, and policy incomplete.
problem.individual_pain1_description: People must cross-reference multiple disconnected sources — calorie trackers, environmental apps, medical guidelines, government recommendations. Over a third of users abandon nutrition apps within months.
problem.individual_pain2_description: People believe they must choose between eating healthy OR eating sustainably. Without integrated data, people operate on misconceptions about which foods are truly optimal on both dimensions.
problem.individual_pain3_description: Only 15% of users sustain dietary behavior change beyond 6 months. Apps fail because they measure inputs without connecting to outcomes — health impact and environmental cost.
problem.researcher_pain1_description: Researchers must pull from 4+ separate, unlinked databases. A Nature Food paper called this 'a fundamental barrier to operationalizing food system monitoring.'
problem.researcher_pain2_description: Each research group uses different LCA methodologies, different nutrient databases, different health models. Results across studies are non-comparable, preventing cumulative knowledge building.
problem.policymaker_pain1_title: Guidelines Built on Half the Picture
problem.policymaker_pain1_description: Dietary guidelines are developed based on nutritional adequacy without systematic environmental impact consideration. A 2024 analysis found policy implementation scores remain 'alarmingly low' for nutrition.
problem.policymaker_pain2_description: EU and Canada use incompatible data systems (HSR vs. Nutri-Score vs. FCS). Environmental LCA is regionalized differently. International collaboration remains aspirational.
problem.synthesis_heading: The World Doesn't Need Another Nutrition App
problem.synthesis_description: It needs the infrastructure layer that connects what we know about food's nutritional value, what we know about food's environmental cost, and what we know about food's clinical health consequences — and makes that integrated knowledge actionable for every person, every researcher, and every policymaker.
problem.synthesis_insight: When a person sees that their dinner scored 3.8 on nutritional quality, would cost their health 0.12 DALYs over a lifetime, and carries a monetized environmental burden of $2.40 CAD — that is a moment that has never existed before in human history.

mission.label: OUR MISSION
mission.heading: Making Environmental Nutrition Accessible to All
mission.mission_statement: NourishSphere exists to build and maintain the world's first open, integrated environmental nutrition intelligence platform — making the connections between food, health, and planet actionable for individuals, researchers, and policymakers worldwide.
mission.vision_statement: A world where every food choice is informed by its full impact — on personal health, community wellbeing, and planetary sustainability.
mission.architecture_description: Like Mozilla Foundation and Firefox, NourishSphere operates a two-brand architecture:
mission.org_role: The non-profit organization — grants, research, policy, legal entity
mission.product_role: The consumer platform — app stores, daily tracking, personal insights
mission.history_description: NourishSphere grew from years of academic research at McGill University, where our founding team recognized that the tools to make truly informed food choices simply didn't exist. The data was there — scattered across nutritional databases, environmental life-cycle assessments, and clinical health models — but no one had integrated it into a single, actionable framework.
mission.value1_description: Our methodologies are transparent, peer-reviewed, and publicly documented.
mission.value2_description: Built for Canada and the EU — English and French are first-class languages.
mission.value3_description: Every claim is backed by validated data, peer-reviewed scoring systems, and reproducible methods.
mission.value4_description: GDPR and Quebec Bill 25 compliant from day one. No advertising, no data selling.

team.label: OUR TEAM
team.heading: Led by Science. Driven by Impact.
team.description: NourishSphere is led by a multidisciplinary team with deep expertise in nutritional science, environmental assessment, and public health policy.
team.founder_bio: Dual-PhD McGill professor specializing in nutritional epidemiology and environmental life-cycle assessment. Over 15 years of research at the intersection of diet, health outcomes, and environmental sustainability.
team.advisory_description: Our advisory board brings together leaders in nutrition science, environmental policy, digital health, and non-profit governance.
team.join_heading: Help Build the Future of Food Intelligence
team.join_description: We're looking for passionate researchers, developers, and communicators who want to make a difference at the intersection of nutrition and sustainability.

methodology.label: METHODOLOGY
methodology.heading: The Science Behind NourishSphere
methodology.description: NourishSphere integrates four validated scoring systems with comprehensive life-cycle assessment data, creating the most complete picture of food's impact available anywhere.
methodology.hsr_description: A front-of-pack labeling system that rates the overall nutritional profile of packaged food on a scale of 0.5 to 5 stars. Considers energy, saturated fat, sodium, sugar, protein, fiber, and fruit/vegetable content.
methodology.fcs_description: Developed by Tufts University, FCS scores foods 1–100 based on 54 attributes across 9 domains including nutrients, food processing, vitamins, and phytochemicals.
methodology.hefi_description: Aligned with Canada's Food Guide, HEFI measures dietary quality based on whole grains, protein foods, fruits & vegetables, unsaturated fats, and limits on sodium, sugars, and saturated fats.
methodology.heni_description: A DALY-based metric that quantifies the health burden of dietary choices. Translates food consumption into disability-adjusted life years — connecting what you eat to clinical health outcomes.
methodology.lca_description: Using ReCiPe 2016 midpoint methodology with Canadian regionalization, NourishSphere evaluates the full environmental footprint of foods across 18 impact categories — from carbon footprint and water use to land occupation and ecotoxicity. All impacts are monetized in CAD for intuitive comparison.
methodology.cnf_description: Health Canada's comprehensive database of nutrient values for 5,000+ foods commonly consumed in Canada.
methodology.gbd_description: The Institute for Health Metrics and Evaluation's database linking dietary risk factors to health outcomes worldwide.

contact.label: GET IN TOUCH
contact.heading: Let's Work Together
contact.description: Whether you're interested in research collaboration, institutional partnerships, or simply want to learn more — we'd love to hear from you.
contact.form_success: Thank you! Your message has been sent. We'll get back to you within 48 hours.

platform.hero_heading: Intelligent Tools for Sustainable Food Decisions
platform.hero_description: EcoDish365 is NourishSphere's consumer platform — human-centric generative AI, a sovereign data pipeline, and privacy-preserving analytics that help you make food decisions backed by science.
platform.cap1_description: Multimodal AI models (LLM + vision + sensor) fine-tuned on privacy-safe nutritional and environmental datasets. Purchasing assistants, preservation advisors, and recipe generators — all with verifiable recommendations via zero-knowledge machine learning (zkML).
platform.cap2_description: A CEADS-aligned, federated data infrastructure that mobilises heterogeneous food-system datasets — personal dietary data, IoT sensors, retail data, sustainability metrics — while guaranteeing full data sovereignty through zero-knowledge proofs and compute-to-data architecture.
platform.cap3_description: Zero-knowledge proof circuits for verifiable aggregation, zkML inference, and sustainability proofs. Personalized AI recommendations without ever exposing raw user data — GDPR, Data Act, and Quebec Bill 25 compliant by design.
platform.cap4_description: Data harmonized through AGROVOC, FoodOn, and SAREF ontologies with EDC/IDS-compatible federated connectors — enabling cross-border, cross-domain research and interoperable Digital Product Passports.
platform.cap5_description: Core platform released under EUPL licence with RESTful API access for research groups. Full contribution back to European data spaces including CEADS, with open weights and inference code for transparency.
platform.cap6_description: Living-lab deployments across multiple countries with government partners, measuring impact through hybrid frameworks — ZKP-verifiable aggregates, IoT sensor data, validated surveys, and independent third-party life-cycle assessment audits.
platform.tool1_description: Generative AI that creates personalized weekly shopping plans optimized for nutrition, sustainability, and budget — factoring in seasonal availability, dietary needs, and household preferences.
platform.tool2_description: Real-time guidance on optimal food storage, shelf-life extension, and preservation techniques — reducing food waste by up to 30% through smart sensor integration and AI predictions.
platform.tool3_description: AI-generated recipes that maximize nutritional quality while minimizing environmental cost. Every recipe shows its carbon score, health impact (DALYs), and monetized environmental burden in CAD.
platform.deployment_description: EcoDish365 is validated through living-lab deployments with government and institutional partners — measuring real impact on health, sustainability, and productivity.
platform.deploy1_description: Generative weekly shopping and preservation plans with zkML recommendations, tested with 200 urban and rural households.
platform.deploy2_description: AI-powered recipe and menu generation with real-time preservation advice and verifiable sustainability scoring for professional food service environments.
platform.deploy3_description: ZKP-based verifiable sustainability claims linked to Digital Product Passports, with AI-driven advice for low-impact production and preservation.
platform.impact_dietary_desc: Improvement in adherence to planetary-health diets
platform.impact_ghg_desc: Lower greenhouse gas emissions per meal
platform.impact_waste_desc: Reduction in food and packaging waste
platform.impact_productivity_desc: Time savings in planning and preparation
platform.cta_heading: Ready to Shape the Future of Food Systems?
platform.cta_description: Whether you're a government body, research institution, SME, or technology partner — join us in building EcoDish365, the sovereign, trustworthy AI platform for sustainable nutrition.
platform.individuals_hero_heading: AI-Powered Food Intelligence for Every Home
platform.individuals_hero_description: EcoDish365's generative AI tools help you purchase smarter, preserve longer, and prepare healthier — all while reducing your environmental footprint with complete privacy.
platform.individuals_privacy_description: All EcoDish365 recommendations are generated using zkML (zero-knowledge machine learning) — meaning the platform can personalize advice without ever seeing your raw dietary data. Full GDPR compliance and data sovereignty guaranteed.
platform.researchers_hero_heading: Sovereign Research Infrastructure for Food Science
platform.researchers_hero_description: EcoDish365's federated, privacy-preserving data pipeline connects nutritional science, environmental LCA, and health outcomes — with open-source tools and API access for reproducible research.
platform.researchers_pipeline_description: EcoDish365's pipeline federates heterogeneous data sources across the computing continuum while guaranteeing full sovereignty through zero-knowledge proofs and compute-to-data architecture.
platform.researchers_feature1_description: EDC/IDS-compatible connectors for in-situ sensors, IoT devices, personal dietary data, retail data, and public government datasets — all processed without centralization.
platform.researchers_feature2_description: Data harmonized through AGROVOC, FoodOn, and SAREF ontologies — enabling cross-border, cross-domain research without manual data cleaning.
platform.researchers_feature3_description: Three verified ZKP circuits: verifiable aggregation (privacy-preserving statistics), zkML inference (AI recommendations without raw data), and sustainability proofs (auditable environmental claims).
platform.researchers_feature4_description: Full platform released under EUPL licence. RESTful API access for research groups. Contribution back to CEADS and EU data spaces.
platform.policymakers_hero_heading: Evidence-Based Policy for Sustainable Food Systems
platform.policymakers_hero_description: EcoDish365 provides validated guidelines, scenario modeling, and cross-border evidence from real-world deployments — enabling integrated dietary-environmental policy backed by verifiable data.
platform.policymakers_output1_description: Handbooks on climate-resilient food practices developed from real-world deployment data, co-created with government stakeholders.
platform.policymakers_output2_description: Evidence-based policy briefs contributed to national ministries, covering data governance, AI integration, and sustainability incentives.
platform.policymakers_output3_description: Third-party LCA audits and ZKP-verifiable aggregates providing independently confirmed improvements in dietary health, GHG emissions, waste, and productivity.
platform.policymakers_output4_description: Interoperable data standards and governance recommendations enabling joint policy action across countries through federated data infrastructure.
